actionable_feedback: Add the missing integrity_action_memo.md file with the exact filename. The memo must include per-feature, row-level citations to packet fields and concise decision rationale (pass/warn/fail/needs_review) for each feature.

If environment parsing might still fail, also attach a CSV export of critical sheets (Inputs, Model, Summary, Sensitivity, Checks) or a plain-text representation of formulas and key computed columns.
Include an Inputs sheet that reproduces the embedded packet values exactly (one row per feature) so reviewers can trace calculations.

Make all formulas visible in the workbook (avoid macros or opaque binary calculations). Provide explicit, cell-level formulas for: immediate-rule evaluation, MAOP and depth risk scoring, 20% pressure-reduction scenario calculations, sensitivity +/- threshold scenarios, and week-by-week scheduling logic.

Add a Checks sheet with validation tests that confirm inputs are reproduced, no externally referenced data is used, and that dig schedule totals respect dig_capacity_week = 2 (week-by-week assignments).

Produce a Sensitivity sheet that shows results for +/- threshold variations, and document how a 20% pressure reduction maps to the reported 25% MAOP risk score reduction (show the intermediate calculation).
Provide agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json that conform to the AgentTrace schema and show the steps taken, tools used, and any errors handled. Preserve failure logs instead of omitting them.
